approval_notes: Owner approved starting F002 with the dev-environment groundwork plus a minimal, live-verified API/PostgreSQL/health vertical slice (prompt: 'do it' on the proposed slice-1 scope).

Technology (FastAPI, SQLAlchemy, Alembic, PostgreSQL, Docker Compose, psycopg, pydantic-settings) is the owner-approved F002 baseline in docs/ARCHITECTURE.md, ADR 0004, and CODEX_TASKS.md task 002, not a new dependency requiring fresh approval.

F002 is an epic; this contract intentionally covers only slice 1. Worker, scheduler, and the React frontend are out of scope for this increment and require an owner checkpoint before slice 2. F002 remains failing until the full stack and Level 2 evaluation land.
objective: Establish repository-owned development-environment documentation and commands, and a minimal but real vertical (FastAPI API + PostgreSQL + Alembic migrations) that starts through one canonical script and is verified live end to end, without adding the worker, scheduler, or frontend yet.
scope: Add docs/LOCAL_DEVELOPMENT.md and docs/CODEX_ENVIRONMENT.md describing the canonical bootstrap, stack, test, and teardown commands and required runtimes.
Add scripts/check-env.ps1 and scripts/check-env.sh that verify Docker, Node, and Python are available and fail actionably when missing, without printing secrets or full environment dumps.
Add scripts/bootstrap-dev.ps1 and scripts/bootstrap-dev.sh that create the Python .venv, install dev and runtime dependencies, and install Node dev dependencies.
Add scripts/test.ps1 and scripts/test.sh that run the pytest suite and delegate to the existing check workflow rather than duplicating hard-coded setup.

Add apps/api: a FastAPI application exposing GET /health (liveness) and GET /health/ready (executes SELECT 1 against PostgreSQL and returns 503 when the database is unreachable), Pydantic settings that read configuration from environment variable names, a Dockerfile, and a README.
Add docker-compose.yml defining postgres and api services with health checks and dependency ordering; web, worker, and scheduler are documented as deferred to slice 2.
Add Alembic configuration (alembic.ini, migrations/env.py, migrations/script.py.mako) and a baseline migration that creates a scaffold app_meta table; the domain model remains F003.
Add .env.example listing required environment variable names with placeholder values only.
Add runtime dependencies to pyproject.toml and a pinned apps/api/requirements.txt used by the Docker image, kept in sync by a test.

Add scripts/stack-up, scripts/stack-down, and scripts/stack-smoke (.ps1 and .sh) to build and start the stack, verify live health and database connectivity and applied migrations, and tear the stack down.
Add pytest unit tests for the API health endpoints and a test asserting the runtime dependency pins stay in sync between pyproject.toml and apps/api/requirements.txt.
out_of_scope: Python worker and scheduler services (F002 slice 2).
React frontend / static web application (F002 slice 2).
Domain model, catalogue tables, evidence history, Z0 classification (F003+).
Configuration system beyond the minimal API settings needed to connect to PostgreSQL (F003).
Merging the pull request, tagging a release, marking F002 passing, or proceeding to F003.
Any external network access from tests or application code, and any use of secrets in CI.
acceptance_criteria: docs/LOCAL_DEVELOPMENT.md and docs/CODEX_ENVIRONMENT.md exist, are non-empty, and document the canonical bootstrap, stack-up, test, stack-smoke, and stack-down commands.
scripts/check-env resolves the repository root from its own path, reports Docker, Node, and Python availability, and exits non-zero with an actionable message when a required runtime is missing.
scripts/bootstrap-dev creates .venv, installs the project with dev extras and runtime dependencies, and installs Node dev dependencies.
scripts/test runs pytest and reports pass/fail; the existing scripts/check.ps1 suite (ruff, ruff format, pytest, prettier, eslint, secret scan, pip-audit) still passes.
docker-compose.yml starts postgres and api; the api container applies Alembic migrations to head on startup and serves /health and /health/ready.

A live run of scripts/stack-up followed by scripts/stack-smoke confirms GET /health returns 200, GET /health/ready returns 200 with the database reachable, and the baseline migration created the app_meta table.
Stopping and restarting the stack (stack-down then stack-up) leaves the database and migrations intact and stack-smoke passes again.

A fresh independent Level 2 evaluator records adversarial end-to-end results (API, database, negative readiness when DB is down, regression) in agent-state/evaluation.json before F002 is considered for any status change.
No secrets are printed or committed; the secret-scan check remains green; .env.example contains only variable names and placeholders.

end_to_end_steps: Run scripts/check-env and confirm Docker, Node, and Python are reported available.
Run scripts/bootstrap-dev and scripts/test and confirm the pytest suite passes.
Run scripts/stack-up, then scripts/stack-smoke, and confirm live /health is 200, /health/ready is 200, and the app_meta table exists.
Stop the api container (or the database) and confirm /health/ready returns 503 while /health stays 200.
Run scripts/stack-down then scripts/stack-up again and confirm stack-smoke still passes with data intact.
negative_tests: With PostgreSQL stopped, GET /health/ready must return 503 with an actionable body and GET /health must still return 200.
scripts/check-env must exit non-zero with an actionable message when a required runtime is simulated missing.
A drift between pyproject.toml runtime pins and apps/api/requirements.txt must fail the sync test.

risks.data_migration: Alembic baseline creates only a scaffold app_meta table; the real domain model arrives in F003. Migrations must apply cleanly from an empty database and be idempotent to head.
risks.security_privacy: Scripts must not print secrets or full environment dumps. .env.example holds names and placeholders only. The database password default is a non-secret local development value documented as such and never used in production.
risks.portability: Scripts must run on Windows PowerShell 7 and POSIX shells and resolve the repository root from their own path. Docker images target linux and remain multi-architecture compatible.
risks.zero_cost_safety: The scaffold runs entirely locally via Docker Compose; no hosting, paid dependency, or free-tier/Z0 claim is introduced.
risks.regression: Runtime dependencies are added without breaking the F001 check suite; migrations and app code live outside the paths formatters curate for docs and agent-state.

notes: Owner approved the slice-1 scope in the current prompt. Scope is bounded to the dev-environment groundwork and a minimal API/PostgreSQL/health vertical; the F002 feature-ledger acceptance steps are unchanged and F002 will not be marked passing by this increment.
